Q16: Yes, I was present and completed the NFL activity.
Q17: My sampler was the same as the third one, but for both spinners the variables were labeled "Team 1" and "Team 2" instead "Win" or "Lose."
Q18:
My chosen sampler has two evenly split 50/50 spinners, one specified for the coin flip and one for the game outcome/game winner. Coin flips are expected to be fair, and either outcome of tails or heads should have a 50% chance of landing, therefore, each team should have a 50% chance of winning the coin flip. I then connected the game outcome spinner to the coin winner spinner so that once the coin winner appeared, the game winner would appear (unrelated to the coin winner though). The branch between the coin winner and game outcome is purely for convenience to get both outcomes at the same time, and these two spinners are practically independent from each other (because I am testing the idea that there is no advantage when winning the coin toss). The game outcome/game winner is also set up with a 50/50 split to show a fair split and probability for each team’s chances of winning. 
I liked labeling the variables for both the coin winner sampler and game winner sampler as Team 1 and Team 2 because I can see which team won the coin flip and which team won the game.
